summary.en: A gritty, creative, and unapologetically independent corridor known for its antique shops, art galleries, and authentic Mexican-American community.
The historic soul of St. Louis, characterized by 19th-century red brick townhomes, cobblestone streets, blues bars, and the legendary Anheuser-Busch Brewery.
An eclectic and energetic six-block entertainment district that buzzes with record stores, diverse restaurants, and iconic music venues.
A sophisticated and stately neighborhood known for its stunning turn-of-the-century architecture, upscale boutiques, and proximity to Forest Park.
A laid-back, foodie-centric neighborhood with a strong sense of community, revolving around Tower Grove Park and the Missouri Botanical Garden.
A culinary crossroads where St. Louis tradition meets the modern artisan, acting as a destination for some of the city's most iconic food spots.

description.en: A beloved neighborhood institution that feels like a cozy, art-filled living room, serving expertly crafted coffee and delicious breakfast/lunch fare.
A dimly lit, eclectic bar with a mystical theme, known for its creative cocktails and laid-back, artsy vibe.
This stretch of Cherokee is St. Louis's 'Little Mexico,' home to spots like Taqueria El Bronco or La Vallesana where the tacos al pastor are legendary.
A small but mighty spot known for its indulgent raclette cheese fries, exemplifying the street's funky, high-low culinary scene.
A dense collection of antique shops filled with unique furniture, oddities, and historical artifacts, making it a paradise for vintage hunters.
The walls and alleys of Cherokee Street are a constantly evolving canvas reflecting the neighborhood's diverse cultural influences and rebellious spirit.
A pilgrimage for beer lovers; head to the Biergarten for a fresh beer in a historic setting surrounded by landmark architecture.
A St. Louis institution with a New Orleans feel, featuring a sprawling patio, nightly live blues music, and authentic bayou cuisine.
The quintessential pre-and-post-Cardinals game bar offering a classic, no-frills St. Louis sports bar experience in the shadow of Busch Stadium.
An authentic 1913 soda fountain that is a delicious step back in time, famous for its massive BLT, thick malts, and house-made candy.
A surrealist jungle gym built from recycled industrial materials where you can climb through salvaged airplanes and slide down multi-story chutes.
An interactive and engaging museum dedicated to the history of the blues, where you can create your own blues mix or play along with a jug band.
Wander the residential side streets to see preserved brick architecture and experience a quiet, historic atmosphere that is a photographer's dream.
A landmark restaurant and bar that's a living museum of pop culture memorabilia, and home to the Duck Room where Chuck Berry played.
A delightfully kitschy time capsule with disco balls, purple lights, and cheap drinks, making it the perfect place for uninhibited dancing.
A local favorite known for its cornmeal-crust deep-dish pizza, with a popular outdoor seating area perfect for people-watching.
A modern, sit-down barbecue joint that elevates the classics, offering top-tier brisket and an impressive whiskey list.
One of the city's best and biggest record shops, it's a must-visit for music lovers looking to browse an enormous collection of new and used LPs.
Embedded in the sidewalk are brass stars and biographical plaques honoring famous St. Louisans, from Tina Turner to T.S. Eliot.
A dynamic, larger-than-life statue of the father of rock 'n' roll in his signature duckwalk pose.
An essential stop on a warm day, this trendy bar specializes in inventive, high-quality frozen cocktails for adults.
One of the best 'drink with a view' spots in the city, where you can rent a paddleboat or just sit by the water with a cocktail.
Authentic French food that feels like a bustling Parisian bistro, with classic steak frites and onion soup.
An airy, beautifully designed restaurant offering an upscale, farm-to-table experience that celebrates the versatility of vegetables.
A breathtakingly serene art space designed by Tadao Ando, where the interplay of concrete, light, and art makes the building a masterpiece.
The grand slope leading down from the Saint Louis Art Museum, offering one of the most iconic and breathtaking views of the city, especially at sunset.
A beautiful, historic structure on a hilltop in Forest Park, offering a fantastic vantage point and a glimpse into the grandeur of the 1904 World's Fair.
Its interior contains one of the largest mosaic collections in the world, a truly awe-inspiring sight that many casual visitors miss.
A beloved family-run restaurant serving authentic Persian food, which also houses a sophisticated gin bar with a massive, expertly curated selection.
One of the city's most celebrated restaurants, serving an innovative and modern take on Mexican flavors in a space that is an explosion of color and design.
A national historic landmark and one of the world's premier botanical gardens, featuring the Climatron conservatory and a serene Japanese Garden.
An incubator for new ideas in the arts, this space hosts contemporary art exhibitions, performances, and workshops.
Walk the streets bordering Tower Grove Park, like Flora Place, to admire stunning Victorian mansions known for their ornate details and vibrant colors.
An award-winning, art deco-style soda fountain famous for its 'ice cream cocktails' and whimsical atmosphere.

name.en: Union Loafers Cafe and Bread Bakery

description.en: A bakery with cult status for its naturally leavened bread and incredible pizzas, embodying the new guard of St. Louis artisans.
Widely considered among the best barbecue in the city, be prepared to wait in line for their legendary Memphis-style ribs.
The city's main arts district, home to numerous cultural institutions whose architecture is worth admiring from the outside.
A magnificent historic theatre in the Grand Center Arts District, whose stunning architecture is worth admiring even from the outside.

prompt.en: Find a piece of street art that speaks to you and take a selfie with it.
Peer into an antique shop window and photograph the most unusual object you can find.
Capture a close-up photo of a texture that represents the neighborhood's gritty charm—think peeling paint, worn brick, or old metal.
Take a photo that shows the fusion of different cultures in one frame.
Find a storefront or sign that perfectly captures the independent, non-corporate vibe of the street.
If you grab a bite, take an artistic, top-down photo of your authentic street taco.
Look down an alleyway and photograph a piece of hidden graffiti or art that most people would miss.
Capture a reflection of the street scene in a quirky shop window.
Find the most vibrant splash of color on a building or mural and make it the focus of your photo.
Photograph a detail that feels uniquely local, whether it's a handmade sign, a community garden, or a local flyer.
Use the afternoon sun to capture an interesting shadow cast by a fire escape, sign, or street artist.
Find the most creatively decorated or weathered doorway and take a photo of it.
Take a photo looking straight up at the mix of old building cornices and modern street wiring.
Take a selfie that captures the punk, rebellious spirit of the neighborhood.
Photograph the most interesting window display you see, whether it's for a gallery, boutique, or bar.
Take a close-up, detailed photo of the historic red brickwork that defines this neighborhood.
Get a low-angle shot of a cobblestone street, emphasizing its texture and history.
Take a selfie in front of a building that looks like it's straight out of the 19th century.
Find a sign or symbol related to music (blues, funk, live music) and capture it in a photo.
Photograph a beautiful piece of decorative ironwork on a fence, gate, or balcony.
Capture a photo that includes a historic architectural detail and a modern element in the same frame.
Take a photo of an inviting patio or beer garden, capturing the social heart of the neighborhood.
Search the brick walls for a faded, painted advertisement from a bygone era (a 'ghost sign') and photograph it.
Take a photo that hints at the area's brewing history, without showing the main brewery itself.
If you stop for a classic St. Louis treat like a malt or gooey butter cake, take a photo that makes it look timeless.
Find an old-fashioned gas lamp or street light and take an atmospheric photo of it.
Find a classic St. Louis Cardinals red or Blues blue color and make it the subject of your photo.
Find a quiet, residential corner and capture a photo that feels peaceful and historic.
Photograph a window that looks like it holds stories from the past.
Find a detail related to old transportation - a carriage house door, a horse tie-ring, or old trolley tracks.
Find a star on the St. Louis Walk of Fame for someone you admire and take a photo of it.
Strike your best rock and roll pose for a selfie somewhere in the Loop.
Capture the vibrant glow of a neon sign from one of the many shops or venues.
Photograph a piece of pop culture memorabilia or a reference you spot in a window display.
Take a photo of a record album cover that catches your eye through a record store window.
Take a photo that captures the energetic, bustling vibe of the street.
Find an example of eclectic fashion or style (on a person or in a shop) and snap a respectful photo.
Take a delicious-looking photo of food being enjoyed on an outdoor patio.
Capture the reflection of the iconic Tivoli Theatre or another landmark in a storefront window.
Photograph the storefront of the most unique or quirky independent shop you can find.
Find a visual pattern or rhythm in the architecture or streetscape and capture it.
Get a creative shot of the trolley tracks running down the center of the street.
As evening approaches, capture a photo of a bar or venue getting ready for the night.
Take a 'from where I stand' photo that includes your shoes and a Walk of Fame star.
Find a restaurant menu displayed outside that has interesting typography or design and photograph it.
Take a photo looking up at the ornate facade of a turn-of-the-century building.
Take a selfie that captures the sophisticated, stately vibe of the neighborhood.
Capture a shot where the lush greenery of Forest Park meets the urban architecture of the CWE.
Photograph the most elegant and artistic window display from an upscale boutique.
Find the most impressive-looking doorway or entrance to a building and take a photo.
Find a piece of public sculpture or modern architecture and take an abstract, close-up photo of its lines and textures.
Capture a candid moment of people enjoying a meal or a drink at a sidewalk cafe.
Take a photo down a quiet, tree-lined residential street, capturing the peaceful atmosphere.
If you're here in the late afternoon, capture how the golden light hits the historic architecture.
Take a photo that could almost be mistaken for a street in a European city.
Find a vantage point and take a wide shot showing the scale of Forest Park.
Find a puddle or a sleek window and capture a reflection of a stately building.
Photograph the exterior of a museum or cultural institution, focusing on a unique architectural detail.
Find one of the neighborhood's many beautiful fountains and take a creative photo of the water.
Spot a well-dressed dog out for a walk and (with permission) snap a photo of the stylish canine.
Find an ornate detail on one of the Victorian 'Painted Ladies' homes and take a close-up shot.
If you eat here, create an artistic 'flatlay' photo of your meal, showcasing the colors and presentation.
Take a photo that is overwhelmingly green, filled with the lush plant life from a local garden or park.
Take a selfie that captures the laid-back, community feel of the neighborhood.
Photograph one of the whimsical, historic pavilions inside Tower Grove Park.
Find the most colorfully painted house or storefront and make it the subject of your photo.
Find a unique flower or plant in a front yard garden and take a macro shot of it.
Photograph a front porch that looks especially welcoming and cozy.
Capture a detail of the unique interior or exterior design of one of the neighborhood's acclaimed restaurants.
Take a photo of a winding path or walkway in Tower Grove Park.
Many homes here have stained glass windows. Find a beautiful example and photograph it.
If there's a farmers market or local grocer, photograph a vibrant display of fresh produce.
Capture the interesting patterns that tree branch shadows make on the side of a brick house.
Find a piece of public art or a small gallery and capture its essence.
Take a photo that represents a moment of relaxation, like a person reading on a bench or an empty hammock.
This is a food destination! Take a photo that breaks down your meal into its delicious components.
Find a building with Art Deco architecture and photograph a classic detail like a geometric pattern or a streamlined curve.
Take a photo that shows the contrast between old St. Louis tradition and new artisan craft.
Capture the impressive marquee or facade of one of the Grand Center Arts District's historic theatres.
Take a selfie with a legendary local dish, like BBQ ribs or an ice cream cocktail.
Take a close-up, mouth-watering shot of the best thing you eat or drink here.
Photograph a detail that showcases artisan craftsmanship, like the crust of a loaf of bread or a perfectly garnished cocktail.
If you see a line for a famous food spot, capture the sense of anticipation and community among the people waiting.
Find a view that frames both a historic building and a modern structure in the same shot.
Take a photo of the exterior of a cultural institution like Powell Hall or the Fox Theatre, focusing on its grandeur.
Find a small park, patio, or green space and capture a moment of peace amidst the urban environment.
Photograph a classic, old-school sign for a restaurant or bar.
Capture a vibrant, colorful photo of a dessert or sweet cocktail.
Stand at the base of a tall building in the arts district and take a perspective shot looking straight up.
If you visit multiple spots, take a photo at each one and create a collage that tells the story of your culinary journey.
